findings.suggestion: Encode output with an HTML encoder or use a template engine that auto-escapes.

findings.title: CWE-330: Predictable PRNG seed at line 187
findings.description: `new Random(42)` at L187 uses a hardcoded seed. This makes the random sequence 100% reproducible by an attacker.
findings.suggestion: Use `new SecureRandom()` which auto-seeds from OS entropy.

findings.title: CWE-330: Weak PRNG (`Random`) in web handler at line 187
findings.description: `new Random()` at L187 in a web-facing method. Weak randomness in web endpoints enables session prediction and CSRF token bypass.
findings.suggestion: Use `java.security.SecureRandom` for web-facing random values.

findings.title: CWE-330: Weak random number generator in `setup()`
findings.description: java.util.Random or Math.random() used — not cryptographically secure.
findings.suggestion: Use java.security.SecureRandom for security-sensitive randomness.

findings.title: CWE-601: Open redirect via sendRedirect in `sendRedirect()`
findings.description: HttpServletResponse.sendRedirect() is called with a URL that may be attacker-influenced.
findings.suggestion: Validate the redirect target against an allowlist or use a mapping instead of user-supplied URLs.
